deprecations.replacement: Use top-level Program.cs with WebApplicationBuilder (ASP.NET Core 6+). ConfigureServices and Configure are replaced by builder.Services and app.Use*.
Use System.Text.Json (built-in since .NET Core 3.0). Configure via builder.Services.AddControllers().AddJsonOptions().
Use WebApplicationBuilder (ASP.NET Core 6+): var builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder(args);
Use services.AddControllers() for API-only projects or services.AddControllersWithViews() for MVC. AddMvc() is a superset that adds unnecessary overhead for APIs.
Use app.MapControllers() with app.UseRouting() and app.UseEndpoints() (ASP.NET Core 3.0+), or simply app.MapControllers() in .NET 6+ minimal hosting.
Use Database.MigrateAsync() in non-development environments. EnsureCreated() bypasses migrations and is only safe for test databases.
Use IHttpClientFactory (registered via services.AddHttpClient()) to manage HttpClient lifetimes and avoid socket exhaustion.
Use AddIdentityApiEndpoints<TUser>() (ASP.NET Core 8+) for token-based auth, or AddIdentityCore<TUser>() for minimal setup without UI.
